metadata.title: 18-Wheeler Accident Lawyer in Wichita Guide | Bull Attorneys
metadata.description: Learn what makes 18-wheeler accident cases different, what evidence matters, common causes, and the steps to take after a truck crash.

author.name: Bradley A. Pistotnik (Trial Lawyer)
author.bio: Brad Pistotnik is the CEO of Bull Attorneys®, P.A. and a Kansas trucking and personal injury lawyer with over 45 years of experience. He focuses on serious injury and truck accident cases involving DOT regulations and commercial vehicle safety standards.

sections.content: A crash with an 18-wheeler can cause major injuries, large medical bills, and life-changing stress. These cases are also more complex — involving trucking companies, commercial insurance, federal safety rules, and several types of evidence.

sections.label: WHAT SETS THESE CASES APART
sections.heading: Why 18-Wheeler Accident Cases Are Different
sections.intro: A regular car accident usually involves two drivers, two insurance companies, and basic evidence. An 18-wheeler case may involve many more parties — and records that only exist in commercial trucking.

sections.label: 2021 LARGE TRUCK CRASH SNAPSHOT
sections.heading: Truck Accident Numbers Show How Serious These Cases Are
sections.intro: Federal truck accident data shows how big the danger is — and why fast action matters.

primaryStats.label: Of fatal large-truck victims were NOT in the truck

of fatal large-truck accidents happened in rural areas

sections.source: Source: FMCSA. Wichita-area truck traffic often connects to major corridors and regional freight routes.

columns.heading: Common Causes of 18-Wheeler Accidents
columns.intro: Sometimes a crash is caused by more than one problem — which is why these cases need careful review.

columns.heading: Evidence That Can Prove What Happened
columns.intro: Truck claims often depend on records that disappear quickly. Early preservation is critical.
items: Truck black box or ECM data

sections.label: WHAT TO DO NOW
sections.heading: After an 18-Wheeler Accident
items: Take photos and video if it is safe
Get witness names and contact information
Do not sign forms from the trucking company
Be careful when speaking with insurance adjusters

sections.heading: Compensation After a Truck Accident
sections.intro: Every case is different. The value depends on injuries, evidence, and available insurance coverage.

faqs.question: What makes an 18-wheeler accident different from a car accident?
faqs.answer: An 18-wheeler accident is often more complex than a car accident because it may involve federal safety rules, trucking company records, commercial insurance, and more than one potentially responsible party.

faqs.question: What should I do after an 18-wheeler accident in Wichita?
faqs.answer: Get medical help, call 911, take photos if it is safe, collect witness information, keep damaged items, and avoid signing forms from the trucking company before getting legal advice.

faqs.question: What evidence matters in a truck accident case?
faqs.answer: Important evidence may include driver logbooks, electronic logging device data, truck black box data, maintenance records, dispatch records, inspection reports, and company safety files.

faqs.question: Why are truck accidents often more serious?
faqs.answer: Large trucks are heavier and larger than passenger vehicles, so crashes often cause more severe injuries, higher medical costs, and more damage.

faqs.question: Can more than one party be responsible for a truck accident?
faqs.answer: Yes. Depending on the case, responsibility may involve the truck driver, trucking company, vehicle owner, maintenance provider, cargo company, or another business.

schema.articleBody: 18-wheeler accidents in Wichita involve multiple parties including the truck driver, trucking company, trailer owner, and cargo loader. These cases require preserving critical evidence like ELD data, driver logbooks, and truck black box records. Federal crash data shows 494,000 large truck crashes occurred in 2021, with 83% of fatal victims being outside the truck. Bull Attorneys in Wichita handles 18-wheeler accident cases with free 24/7 consultations and no fee unless they win.
